2.7 Directional Derivative

In gradient and partial derivatives, we considered the change of the function along the direction of , , , etc. Now, we consider the change in the function along a customized, non-coordinate direction.

In other words, fix a real function , and a two dimensional vector in the x-y plane , which is parameterized by . , what is

Definition 2.7.1.

The directional derivative of the function wrt arc-length in the direction of is

In other words, is the slope of the slice of graph by a vertical plane parallel to .

With chain rule,

As a quick sanity check,

Furthermore,

Since , this implies the increment of the function is maximized along the direction of the gradient, minimized in the opposite direction as the gradient. Also, is tangent to level.
